headline: A Surgeon's Quiet Revolution: Healing Hidden Wounds
meta_description: Senegalese surgeon Professor Serigne Magueye Gueye wins UN Population Award for restoring health and dignity to women living with obstetric fistula.
summary_short: A Senegalese surgeon who transformed his home into a health center wins the UN Population Award for treating obstetric fistula and training surgeons across Africa.
body:
Behind each operation, there is a story. That is how Professor Serigne Magueye Gueye describes his life's work—restoring women's dignity after childbirth injuries that often leave them incontinent, isolated, and rejected by their communities.

This week, the Senegalese surgeon received the 2026 United Nations Population Award, recognizing decades spent not only performing life-changing surgeries but training specialists across more than 45 countries in sub-Saharan Africa. Yet when speaking to UN News, he deflected praise, calling the award "collective work" rather than personal recognition.

What makes his commitment distinctive is its reach beyond operating theatres. Professor Gueye transformed his own home into the Aristide Mensah Health Center in Yeumbeul, on Dakar's outskirts, bringing care to underserved communities. He has also championed awareness of obstetric fistula across West Africa—a largely preventable injury that remains hidden due to community stigma.

He stressed that treatment extends far beyond surgery. Communities must identify affected women, support them through recovery, and help them rebuild their lives socially and economically. His message to young health professionals was clear: develop skills with humility while reaching beyond urban hospitals to remote populations where healthcare remains scarce.

The award also recognized Burundi's Fondation Bonne Action Umugiraneza, which opened the Polyclinique Umugiraneza in Gitega Province, bringing specialist services to rural communities. Together, these laureates embody a fundamental truth: health, autonomy, and dignity form the foundation of resilient societies.
